_comment: IRE Identification Rules for Avennorth Pathfinder SGC. These rules define how incoming CI data from the Pathfinder Gateway is matched against existing CMDB records during Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE) processing.

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder Server Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies servers observed by Pathfinder agents. Matches on hostname and primary IP address. Servers are the foundational CIs discovered through eBPF/ETW kernel observation.

match_fields.description: Server hostname as reported by the Pathfinder agent
Primary IP address observed by the agent
Hardware serial number when available from agent inventory

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder Application Instance Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies application server instances discovered through process-port inference. Uses a composite key of application name, host reference, and TCP port to uniquely identify each instance.

match_fields.description: Application instance name inferred from process name and classification engine
Reference to the hosting server CI
Primary listening TCP port for this application instance

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder Cloud Service Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies cloud services discovered through outbound traffic analysis. Pathfinder agents observe outbound connections and the classification engine maps destination IPs and TLS SNI values to known cloud service endpoints.

match_fields.description: Cloud service name (e.g., 'AWS S3', 'Azure Blob Storage')

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder Medical Device Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies medical devices discovered through healthcare protocol analysis (HL7, FHIR, DICOM). Uses serial number as primary identifier with MAC+IP fallback for devices that do not report serial numbers.

match_fields.description: Device serial number extracted from protocol metadata
Device MAC address observed on the network
Device IP address observed on the network

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder IoT Device Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies IoT and IP-connected devices discovered through IoT protocol analysis (MQTT, CoAP, AMQP). Uses MAC address and IP address as the composite key since IoT devices rarely have meaningful hostnames.

match_fields.description: Device MAC address observed on the network
Device IP address observed on the network

identification_rules.name: Pathfinder Relationship Identification
identification_rules.description: Identifies relationships between CIs discovered by Pathfinder. Relationships are observed at the network level (TCP connections, protocol exchanges) and classified by the gateway engine.

match_fields.description: Source CI of the relationship (upstream dependency)
Target CI of the relationship (downstream consumer)
Relationship type (e.g., 'Depends on::Used by', 'Sends data to::Receives data from')
